title: Women, Business and the Law (WBL) 2026 Course: Advancing Global Gender Equality Through Legal Reform and Data-Driven Policy
summary: Despite major global progress in education, employment, and entrepreneurship, a striking reality remains: no country in the world currently provides women and men with fully equal legal rights . This gap in legal equality continues to restrict women’s economic participation, limits entrepreneurship opportunities, and slows down global economic growth. To address this challenge, the Women, Business and the Law (WBL) 2026 course invites policymakers, researchers, and development professionals to explore global data, legal frameworks, and evidence-based policy solutions aimed at closing the gender equality gap. This online learning program is built around the World Bank’s Women, Business and the Law index, which examines how legal barriers and implementation gaps affect women’s economic opportunities across 190 economies worldwide . About the Women, Business and the Law (WBL) Index The WBL index is a globally recognized research framework that measures how laws impact women’s economic inclusion. It focuses on: Legal rights affecting women’s work and entrepreneurship Barriers in employment, pay, and mobility Laws affecting marriage, parenthood, and pensions Enforcement gaps between law and implementation Cross-country legal comparisons across 190 economies By analyzing these dimensions, the WBL framework provides policymakers with evidence needed to design effective gender equality reforms. Course Overview: Women, Business and the Law 2026 The Women, Business and the Law 2026 course is an e-learning program designed to equip participants with the knowledge and tools needed to understand, analyze, and apply gender equality data in policymaking. Key Course Details Duration: 15 hours total learning time Video content: 2 hours Format: Online e-learning platform Community access: Multiple discussion forums Assessment: Included Certification: Available upon completion Enrollment status: Open Start date for registered users: 23 June 2026 What You Will Learn The course is designed to build both analytical and practical policy skills. Participants will learn how to: 1. Understand Legal Barriers to Women’s Economic Participation Identify laws that restrict women’s access to economic opportunities Explore key legal constraints across different sectors Analyze gender gaps in legal systems globally 2. Apply the Women, Business and the Law Index Understand the methodology behind the WBL index Learn how data is collected and interpreted Explore the index’s three core pillars 3. Use Data for Policy Development Translate research findings into actionable policy recommendations Support government reforms using data-driven evidence Improve decision-making in ministries and public institutions 4. Analyze Global and Regional Trends Compare legal frameworks across countries Identify regions with strongest and weakest performance Understand reform patterns over time Course Structure and Modules The course is structured into five key learning sections: Module 1: Introduction to the Course Welcome overview Pre-course assessment Introduction to WBL framework Keynote sessions Forum discussions Module 2: Understanding the WBL Index Global trends from WBL 2026 Three pillars of the index Knowledge checks and readings Module 3: Women’s Rights Across the Life Cycle Legal rights from employment to retirement Deep dives into weak-performing legal areas Reform case studies and podcasts Module 4: Using WBL Data Exploring WBL databases Economy profiles and reform tracking Policy application and decision-making tools Module 5: Course Completion Final assessment Certification Feedback and next steps Who Should Enroll in This Course? This course is designed for professionals involved in policy, governance, research, and development. Target Participants Include: Government officials Central bank and financial sector staff Ministry employees (Economy, Finance, Gender, Social Protection, Labor) Policy analysts and advisors Legal professionals Economists and researchers Civil society organizations (CSOs) Academic professionals Learning Outcomes By completing this course, participants will be able to: Identify key legal constraints affecting women’s economic participation Use global datasets to inform policy decisions Develop evidence-based gender equality reforms Strengthen cross-country comparative analysis skills Improve policy briefing and advisory capabilities Course Benefits Participants will gain: Access to world-class gender equality data Practical policy design skills Internationally recognized learning certification Networking opportunities through discussion forums Exposure to global best practices in legal reform Why This Course Is Important Gender inequality in law remains one of the biggest barriers to inclusive economic growth. By equipping professionals with data-driven tools and legal insights, the Women, Business and the Law 2026 course helps strengthen: Economic inclusion Women’s empowerment National policy reform Sustainable development goals (SDGs) Enrollment Information Enrollment: Open now Start Date: 23 June 2026 Click here for more and to apply Final Note This course provides a rare opportunity to engage with global gender equality data and transform it into meaningful policy action. Whether you are a policymaker, researcher, or development practitioner, this program equips you with the tools needed to help close the gender gap in economic opportunity worldwide. Click here for more global Training Programs The post Women, Business and the Law (WBL) 2026 Course: Advancing Global Gender Equality Through Legal Reform and Data-Driven Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNIDO-WTCA Blue Economy Global Call 2026: Win Global Recognition, Acceleration Support, and Scale Your Ocean Innovation Worldwide
summary: Innovators, entrepreneurs, researchers, startups, companies, NGOs, and institutions working on sustainable ocean and water solutions now have an exciting opportunity to showcase their innovations on a global stage. The UNIDO-WTCA Blue Economy Global Call 2026 is officially accepting applications from organizations and innovators developing transformative solutions that contribute to a more sustainable, resilient, and inclusive Blue Economy. Organized by the United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO) Investment and Technology Promotion Office Italy and the World Trade Centers Association (WTCA), this international competition seeks innovative technologies and scalable business solutions capable of addressing some of the world s most pressing ocean, coastal, and freshwater challenges. Selected winners will gain access to a prestigious acceleration program, international visibility, expert mentoring, business matchmaking opportunities, potential pilot projects, and global networking opportunities through UNIDO and WTCA s extensive international ecosystem. Understanding the Blue Economy The Blue Economy represents all economic activities linked to oceans, seas, rivers, lakes, and other water resources while ensuring environmental sustainability and ecosystem protection. According to the OECD, the Blue Economy generated approximately $1.5 trillion globally in 2010 and is projected to: Double in size by 2030. Reach approximately $5 trillion in gross value added by 2050. Support the livelihoods of more than 3 billion people worldwide. Beyond its economic significance, the Blue Economy plays a critical role in: Climate regulation. Biodiversity conservation. Food security. Coastal protection. Sustainable development. Renewable energy generation. As global demand for sustainable development increases, innovative solutions are becoming essential to balance economic growth with environmental protection. About the UNIDO-WTCA Blue Economy Global Call 2026 The Global Call aims to identify, support, and accelerate innovative solutions that contribute to sustainable Blue Economy development. The initiative specifically seeks: Ready-to-deploy technologies. Scalable innovations. Sustainable business models. High-impact solutions. Inclusive development initiatives. Climate-resilient technologies. Particular emphasis will be placed on innovations that benefit: Developing countries. Economies in transition. Small Island Developing States (SIDS). Coastal communities. Vulnerable populations dependent on marine and freshwater resources. Six Strategic Categories Applicants may select up to two categories when submitting their proposal. 1. Blue Renewable Energy Solutions that promote sustainable energy generation from marine and aquatic resources, including: Offshore wind energy. Wave energy technologies. Tidal power systems. Ocean thermal energy solutions. Hybrid renewable energy systems. 2. Sustainable Blue Food Innovations that support responsible food production from aquatic environments, including: Sustainable aquaculture. Fisheries innovation. Seafood traceability technologies. Alternative aquatic food systems. Smart fishery management solutions. 3. Green Maritime Transport and Smart Ports Solutions that reduce environmental impacts across maritime logistics and shipping sectors, including: Green shipping technologies. Smart port systems. Maritime digitalization. Emission reduction solutions. Sustainable logistics innovations. 4. Coastal and Marine Tourism Recreation Solutions that promote sustainable tourism while protecting marine ecosystems and maximizing economic benefits for local communities. Potential focus areas include: Eco-tourism technologies. Sustainable destination management. Coastal conservation initiatives. Community-based tourism models. Digital tools for responsible tourism. 5. Sea, Lakes and River Health Restoration Innovations designed to restore and protect aquatic ecosystems, including: Water restoration technologies. Pollution monitoring systems. Plastic waste reduction solutions. Ecosystem rehabilitation initiatives. Biodiversity protection technologies. 6. Blue Tech Biotechnology Advanced technologies and scientific innovations supporting sustainable aquatic development, including: Marine biotechnology. Ocean data analytics. Artificial intelligence applications. Bio-based solutions. Marine resource optimization technologies. Why Participate? The UNIDO-WTCA Blue Economy Global Call 2026 offers significantly more than recognition. Winning projects will receive access to a comprehensive acceleration ecosystem designed to help innovations scale internationally. Benefits for Winners Each category winner will enter a dedicated acceleration program under the UNIDO-WTCA umbrella and receive: UNIDO Support Technical cooperation expertise. Promotion in partner countries. Potential pilot and implementation opportunities. Access to development cooperation networks. WTCA Support Global business development platforms. International market access opportunities. Client and investor matchmaking. Business acceleration support. Worldwide visibility through WTCA s network. Strategic Growth Support High-level mentoring. Expert guidance. Scaling support. International networking opportunities. One year of post-award support. Investment and Capital-Raising Opportunities In addition to category winners, up to ten shortlisted applicants in each category will receive support through: Virtual demo days. In-person investor showcases. Investment portfolio publications. Exposure to development finance institutions. Venture capital networks. Impact investors. Commercial banks. Philanthropic organizations. Travel Support Winning teams attending the Award Ceremony in Rome may receive reimbursement of up to USD $3,000 for eligible travel and accommodation expenses associated with participation in the event. Who Can Apply? The Global Call welcomes applications from: Startups. MSMEs. Large companies. Universities. Research institutions. NGOs. Public-private partnerships. Foundations. Innovation initiatives. Technology developers. Applicants may submit: Early-stage innovations. Pilot projects. Scaling solutions. Technologies undergoing testing. Proven commercial innovations. Eligibility Requirements To qualify, applicants must meet the following criteria: Applicants must be at least 18 years old. Applications must be submitted in English. Applicants may select up to two categories. Projects must demonstrate a Technology Readiness Level (TRL) of 4 or higher. Applications must be complete and aligned with the objectives of the competition. Solutions must demonstrate feasibility and credibility. Entries must be original and owned by the applicant. No modifications can be made after submission. Evaluation Criteria Applications will be assessed by a Scientific Committee and an International Jury based on the following criteria: Evaluation Area Weight Innovative Content 25% Team Competitiveness 20% Scalability Potential 15% Technology Readiness Level 10% Environmental, Social Economic Impact 10% Expected Employment Generation 10% Financial Sustainability 10% Timeline Application Opening 20 April 2026 Application Deadline 19 June 2026 Evaluation and Selection Period June – July 2026 Award Ceremony and Showcase 1 October 2026 The award ceremony will take place in Rome, Italy during the Euro Mediterranean Water Forum. How to Apply Click here for more and to apply Why This Opportunity Matters As oceans, rivers, lakes, and coastal ecosystems face increasing environmental pressures, innovative solutions are essential for building a sustainable future. The UNIDO-WTCA Blue Economy Global Call 2026 represents a unique opportunity for innovators worldwide to showcase their technologies, gain international recognition, connect with investors, and accelerate solutions that can improve livelihoods while protecting critical aquatic ecosystems. For startups, researchers, NGOs, and enterprises working to transform the Blue Economy, this competition offers a powerful platform to scale impact globally. Application Deadline: 19 June 2026 Click here for more global Competition opportunities The post UNIDO-WTCA Blue Economy Global Call 2026: Win Global Recognition, Acceleration Support, and Scale Your Ocean Innovation Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Mixed Methods Researcher at SaverLife: Join a Mission-Driven Team Transforming Financial Futures Across America
summary: Are you passionate about uncovering meaningful insights through research and transforming data into stories that inspire action? SaverLife is currently seeking a talented Mixed Methods Researcher to join its growing team. This exciting opportunity is ideal for professionals who thrive at the intersection of science, storytelling, analytics, and human-centered impact. As a mission-driven nonprofit organization, SaverLife is dedicated to helping individuals and families save more money, reduce financial stress, and build stronger financial futures. The organization is now looking for a researcher who can lead projects from inception to completion, turning complex research questions into actionable insights that support positive change. If you enjoy designing studies, analyzing data, uncovering trends, and communicating findings in compelling ways, this role offers an opportunity to make a meaningful impact while working with a purpose-driven organization. About SaverLife SaverLife is a nonprofit organization focused on empowering individuals to achieve greater financial stability and resilience. Through research, innovation, and member-focused programs, the organization helps people develop stronger savings habits and build brighter futures. The SaverLife team is united by a common mission: helping members save more, worry less, and improve their financial well-being. The organization values curiosity, ambition, agility, and a people-first mindset in everything it does. By joining SaverLife, employees become part of a collaborative community that is committed to creating measurable social impact. Mixed Methods Researcher Position Overview The Mixed Methods Researcher role is designed for professionals who can seamlessly blend qualitative and quantitative research approaches. SaverLife describes the ideal candidate as someone who is equally a scientist and a storyteller. This position involves managing the entire research lifecycle, from identifying research questions to presenting findings and recommendations to stakeholders. Researchers in this role will be expected to: Design rigorous and evidence-based research studies. Collect and analyze both qualitative and quantitative data. Work with complex and sometimes messy datasets. Extract meaningful insights from research findings. Translate data into compelling narratives. Present findings to stakeholders in a clear and actionable manner. Manage multiple research projects from start to finish. Adapt quickly as organizational priorities evolve. Ensure accuracy, quality, and attention to detail throughout the research process. What Makes This Opportunity Unique? Unlike many research positions that focus solely on one stage of the research process, this role offers ownership of the entire project lifecycle. Successful candidates will have the opportunity to: 1. Lead Research from Start to Finish Researchers will be responsible for: Defining research objectives. Designing methodologies. Conducting data collection. Performing analysis. Creating reports. Delivering presentations and recommendations. 2. Influence Decision-Making The insights generated through research will directly contribute to organizational strategies and programs that support financial well-being for SaverLife members. 3. Combine Science and Storytelling The role is ideal for professionals who enjoy both technical research work and communicating findings in ways that inspire action. 4. Work in a Mission-Driven Environment Employees contribute to a meaningful cause that helps people improve their financial security and quality of life. The Ideal Candidate SaverLife is searching for individuals who: Love asking questions and exploring complex problems. Enjoy both qualitative and quantitative research methods. Are comfortable working with ambiguity. Can manage competing priorities effectively. Value precision and attention to detail. Communicate findings clearly to diverse audiences. Thrive in collaborative environments. Have a strong passion for social impact and evidence-based decision-making. Candidates who enjoy uncovering patterns in data and translating them into practical recommendations will find this position especially rewarding. Benefits of Working at SaverLife SaverLife believes that a rewarding career should support both professional development and personal well-being. As a remote-first organization, SaverLife provides a comprehensive benefits package that supports employees in multiple aspects of their lives. Health and Wellness Benefits Employees receive: Full medical coverage. Full dental coverage. Full vision coverage. 50% family coverage support. Work-Life Balance Benefits SaverLife offers: Flexible work schedules. Alternating Flex Fridays off. Generous vacation leave. Generous sick leave. 11 paid holidays annually. Professional Development Opportunities Employees receive: $1,000 annual professional development stipend. Opportunities to enhance research and professional skills. Support for continuous learning and career growth. Financial Benefits SaverLife provides: Competitive compensation. 401(k) matching up to 5% annually. Remote Work Support To help employees build productive home workspaces, SaverLife offers: $500 home office setup stipend. SaverLife s Core Values The organization’s culture is built around four key values: People First Prioritizing the needs and experiences of both members and team members. Agile By Design Remaining adaptable and responsive to changing needs and opportunities. Curious Encouraging continuous learning, exploration, and innovation. Ambitious Pursuing meaningful impact through bold goals and measurable outcomes. These values guide decision-making, collaboration, and organizational growth. Why Consider This Role? The Mixed Methods Researcher position presents a rare opportunity to: Conduct meaningful research that influences real-world outcomes. Work with a mission-driven nonprofit organization. Enjoy the flexibility of a remote-first work environment. Receive strong benefits and professional development support. Lead impactful projects from concept to completion. Collaborate with a team committed to improving financial well-being nationwide. For professionals who are passionate about research, storytelling, and social impact, this position offers a unique platform to make a difference while advancing their careers. Application Information Interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications as soon as possible. Click here for more and to apply Candidates should review the complete job description and application requirements before submitting their applications. Click here for more global job opportunities. The post Mixed Methods Researcher at SaverLife: Join a Mission-Driven Team Transforming Financial Futures Across America appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: SHE-CREEDS Intra-Africa Academic Mobility Scholarships 2026: Fully Funded MSc and PhD Opportunities for African Students in Climate-Responsive Agriculture, Energy-Efficient Technologies, and Digital Innovation
summary: African students seeking world-class postgraduate education, international academic exposure, and the opportunity to contribute to sustainable development now have an exceptional opportunity through the SHE-CREEDS Intra-Africa Academic Mobility Scholarships 2026. Co-funded by the European Union under the Intra-Africa Academic Mobility Scheme (NDICI-2024-MOBAF), the SHE-CREEDS project is offering fully supported Master’s and PhD scholarship opportunities for qualified students from Sub-Saharan Africa. The initiative is designed to strengthen higher education systems, promote academic mobility, foster regional integration, and equip future leaders with the knowledge and skills needed to address climate change, food security, renewable energy challenges, and digital transformation across Africa. The scholarship programme is coordinated by the Nelson Mandela African Institution of Science and Technology (NM-AIST) in Tanzania and implemented through a consortium of leading universities across Eastern, Central, Southern, and Western Africa, with technical support from Ghent University in Belgium and collaboration from the Council for Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) in Ghana. About the SHE-CREEDS Project SHE-CREEDS stands for: Strengthening Higher Education for Climate-Responsive Agriculture, Energy-Efficient Technologies, and Digital Innovation in Sub-Saharan Africa. The project is a four-year initiative running from 2025 to 2028 under the Intra-Africa Academic Mobility Scheme managed by the European Education and Culture Executive Agency (EACEA) on behalf of the European Union. The programme seeks to strengthen the capacity of African higher education institutions to respond effectively to climate change and sustainable development challenges through postgraduate education, research, innovation, and cross-border academic mobility. The project focuses on: Mainstreaming climate change into postgraduate education. Improving the quality of training in Climate-Responsive Agriculture. Advancing Energy-Efficient Technologies. Accelerating digitalisation in higher education. Promoting regional integration among African universities. Strengthening food security and sustainable agriculture. Supporting renewable energy development. Enhancing graduate employability and green skills. Building a resilient and environmentally sustainable Africa. Through academic mobility and knowledge exchange, SHE-CREEDS aims to create a new generation of African researchers, innovators, and professionals capable of driving the continent’s green transition. Why the SHE-CREEDS Scholarship Matters Africa faces growing challenges related to climate change, environmental sustainability, food security, energy access, and technological advancement. Addressing these challenges requires highly trained professionals, researchers, and innovators equipped with interdisciplinary knowledge and practical skills. The SHE-CREEDS programme responds directly to these needs by investing in advanced postgraduate education and supporting mobility between African institutions. Scholars gain access to diverse academic environments, international networks, advanced research facilities, and opportunities to collaborate across borders. The programme aligns with the European Union’s Global Gateway priorities and supports long-term capacity building across African higher education institutions. Scholarship Opportunities Available PhD Scholarships The programme offers six PhD scholarship opportunities. Available PhD Programmes NM-AIST, Tanzania PhD in Sustainable Agriculture Focus: Agro-Ecological Farming Systems and Sustainable Land Management Credit-Seeking Mobility (6 Months) Jimma University, Ethiopia PhD in Natural Resources Management Specialisation: Climate Change Adaptation and Mitigation Focus: Smart Irrigation, Water-Energy-Food Nexus, and Precision Agriculture Technologies Degree-Seeking Université Officielle de Bukavu (UOB), Democratic Republic of Congo PhD in Environmental Science – Natural Resource Management Focus: Agro-Industrial Waste Valorisation Biodiversity-Positive Farming Sustainable Mining for Land Rehabilitation in the Albertine Rift Degree-Seeking Université Félix Houphouët-Boigny (UFHB), Côte d’Ivoire PhD in Climate Change and Biodiversity Focus: Climate-Smart Cocoa Landscapes Degree-Seeking NM-AIST, Tanzania PhD in Hydrology and Water Resources Engineering Focus: Smart Irrigation Water-Energy-Food Nexus Precision Agriculture Technologies Credit-Seeking Mobility (6 Months) NM-AIST, Tanzania PhD in Sustainable Energy Science and Engineering Focus: Renewable Energy Systems Green Manufacturing Solar-Driven Technologies Degree-Seeking University of Zambia (UNZA), Zambia PhD in Integrated Water Resources Management Focus: Energy Efficiency in Transport Water Infrastructure Urban Systems Energy-Efficient Water Systems Degree-Seeking MSc Scholarships The programme offers Master s scholarship opportunities in multiple specialised fields. Available MSc Programmes NM-AIST, Tanzania MSc in Sustainable Agriculture Focus: Agro-Ecological Farming Systems and Sustainable Land Management Jimma University, Ethiopia MSc in Agronomy Focus: Agrifood Value Chain Development Rural SME Empowerment Université Officielle de Bukavu (DRC) MSc in Natural Resource Management Focus: Agro-Industrial Waste Valorisation Biodiversity-Positive Farming Sustainable Mining for Land Rehabilitation Université Félix Houphouët-Boigny, Côte d’Ivoire MSc in Climate Change, Biodiversity and Sustainable Agriculture Focus: Agro-Ecological Farming Systems Sustainable Land Management University of Zambia Master of Science in Geology Focus: Mineral Systems Hydrogeology Geological Hazard Assessment NM-AIST, Tanzania MSc in Sustainable Energy Science and Engineering Focus: Renewable Energy Systems Green Manufacturing Solar-Driven Technologies Scholarship Benefits Successful candidates will receive comprehensive support throughout their studies. Financial Benefits MSc Scholars Monthly stipend of up to EUR 890. Funding duration of up to 24 months for degree-seeking programmes. PhD Scholars Monthly stipend of up to EUR 1,230. Funding duration of up to 36 months for degree-seeking programmes. Funding duration of up to 6 months for credit-seeking mobility. Additional Benefits International academic mobility opportunities. Access to leading African universities. Research collaboration opportunities. Exposure to multicultural academic environments. Professional networking opportunities. Enhanced employability and leadership development. Participation in research addressing real African development challenges. Opportunities to contribute to climate resilience, renewable energy development, and digital innovation. Partner Universities and Institutions The SHE-CREEDS consortium consists of distinguished institutions across Africa and Europe. Consortium Members Nelson Mandela African Institution of Science and Technology (NM-AIST), Tanzania – Lead Coordinator. Jimma University (JU), Ethiopia. Université Officielle de Bukavu (UOB), Democratic Republic of Congo. Université Félix Houphouët-Boigny (UFHB), Côte d’Ivoire. University of Zambia (UNZA), Zambia. Ghent University (UGent), Belgium – EU Technical Partner. Council for Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR), Ghana – Associated Partner. Cross-Language Mobility Advantage The programme encourages linguistic diversity and regional integration. Applicants from Anglophone countries applying to Francophone institutions (UOB or UFHB) receive a cross-language selection advantage. Similarly, applicants from Francophone countries applying to Anglophone institutions may benefit from this initiative. Language support services will be available upon arrival at host institutions. Important Mobility Requirement Applicants cannot study in their country of nationality or residence. The scholarship is specifically designed to promote cross-border academic mobility. Therefore: The host institution must be located in a different African country from the applicant s nationality. The host institution must also be located outside the applicant s country of residence. Applications proposing in-country mobility will be automatically disqualified. Gender Inclusion and Support SHE-CREEDS strongly promotes gender equality. The project aims to achieve at least 50% female scholarship recipients and offers: A 5-point bonus during selection. Dedicated mentorship programmes. Female peer-support networks. Family visit allowances for degree-seeking PhD scholars. Flexible arrangements for nursing mothers. Priority Groups Priority consideration is given to: Applicants from disadvantaged socio-economic backgrounds. Applicants from conflict-affected and fragile regions. Underrepresented groups in STEM fields. Cross-language applicants. Persons with disabilities requiring accessible support. Application Process Applicants should follow these steps: Step 1 Review available MSc and PhD programmes and identify suitable opportunities. Step 2 Prepare all required supporting documents in PDF format. Step 3 Complete the official online application form. Step 4 Submit the application before the deadline. Step 5 Participate in a virtual interview if shortlisted. Step 6 Await final selection results, expected by July 2026. Required Application Documents All Applicants Completed online application. Curriculum Vitae (CV). Motivation Letter. Valid Passport. National Identity Card. Academic Certificates. Academic Transcripts. Language Proficiency Evidence (where applicable). Signed Declaration confirming no concurrent EU scholarship and no previous MOBAF scholarship. Additional Documents for MSc Applicants Two Academic or Professional Reference Letters. Statement of Purpose. Additional Documents for PhD Applicants Research Concept Note (1,000–1,500 words). Two Academic or Professional Reference Letters. Research Publications, Reports, or Conference Papers (if available). Application Deadline The deadline for submitting applications is: 28 June 2026 at 23:59 GMT Late or incomplete applications will not be considered. How to Apply To learn more and apply: Read the official call for application The SHE-CREEDS scholarship programme represents one of Africa’s most significant postgraduate mobility initiatives, offering students the opportunity to study across borders, engage in transformative research, and become leaders in climate-responsive agriculture, energy-efficient technologies, and digital innovation. Interested candidates are encouraged to prepare their applications early and submit before the official deadline. For more information, visit the official website Discover more global scholarship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post SHE-CREEDS Intra-Africa Academic Mobility Scholarships 2026: Fully Funded MSc and PhD Opportunities for African Students in Climate-Responsive Agriculture, Energy-Efficient Technologies, and Digital Innovation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Next Gen Campus Journalism Clinic 2026 for Student Journalists in Ghana and The Gambia: Fully-Focused Training Opportunity for Emerging Media Leaders
summary: The Centre for Journalism Innovation and Development (CJID) has officially opened applications for the 2026 Next Gen Campus Journalism Clinic , an exciting capacity-building opportunity designed for aspiring student journalists in Ghana and The Gambia . Organized through the Next Gen (Campus Reporter) Project , this initiative seeks to equip young journalists, storytellers, communicators, and campus media practitioners with the practical skills, ethical foundations, and digital tools needed to excel in today s rapidly evolving media landscape. The programme aims to nurture a new generation of media professionals capable of producing impactful journalism that promotes accountability, inclusion, democratic values, transparency, and social development across their campuses and communities. The application deadline is Monday, 8 June 2026 . About the Next Gen (Campus Reporter) Project The Next Gen Project , also known as the Campus Reporter Project, is a learning and mentorship platform developed by the Centre for Journalism Innovation and Development . The initiative focuses on empowering young media practitioners across tertiary institutions in West Africa by providing training, mentorship, and exposure to modern journalism practices. The programme recognizes the growing importance of: Ethical journalism Digital storytelling Data-driven reporting Fact-checking Investigative journalism Multimedia communication Information integrity By strengthening these competencies, the project helps young journalists become more effective contributors to democratic governance and community development. Why the Next Gen Campus Journalism Clinic Matters The media landscape is changing rapidly due to technological advancements, social media expansion, misinformation challenges, and the growing demand for credible information. Student journalists often serve as important voices within their institutions and local communities. However, many lack access to specialized training and mentorship opportunities. The Next Gen Campus Journalism Clinic addresses this gap by providing participants with practical skills that will help them: Produce accurate and ethical journalism Combat misinformation and disinformation Amplify underrepresented voices Use digital tools effectively Develop investigative reporting skills Create impactful multimedia content Promote accountability and transparency Training Focus Areas Selected participants will undergo intensive practical training covering several critical journalism competencies. 1. Journalism Ethics and Responsible Reporting Participants will learn the principles that guide professional journalism, including: Accuracy Fairness Objectivity Accountability Public interest reporting The training will emphasize responsible reporting practices that strengthen public trust in journalism. 2. Gender-Sensitive Reporting and Inclusive Storytelling The programme will help participants understand how to report stories in ways that: Promote inclusion Reduce harmful stereotypes Reflect diverse perspectives Elevate marginalized voices Participants will learn techniques for producing more equitable and representative journalism. 3. Data Journalism and Story Interpretation Data is becoming increasingly important in modern journalism. Participants will learn how to: Analyze data Interpret statistics Identify trends Use data to support stories Present complex information clearly These skills can help journalists create evidence-based reporting that informs audiences effectively. 4. Open Source Intelligence (OSINT) Techniques OSINT has become a vital tool for journalists worldwide. Participants will be introduced to methods for: Gathering publicly available information Verifying online content Conducting digital investigations Monitoring developments across multiple platforms These skills are increasingly valuable for investigative and digital reporting. 5. Fact-Checking and Verification In an era of misinformation and disinformation, verification skills are essential. The programme will train participants to: Verify information sources Identify misleading content Conduct fact-checking investigations Strengthen information integrity These competencies help ensure accuracy and credibility in journalism. 6. Optimal Use of Social Media and Search Engines for Story Amplification Participants will learn strategies for: Growing audience engagement Increasing story visibility Using search engines effectively Leveraging social media platforms professionally Expanding the reach of impactful journalism This training helps journalists maximize the impact of their reporting. 7. Digital Storytelling and Multimedia Reporting Modern audiences consume content across multiple formats. Participants will gain practical knowledge in: Multimedia journalism Visual storytelling Digital content creation Interactive reporting Audience-centered communication These skills can help student journalists adapt to contemporary media environments. 8. Reporting on Governance, Human Rights, and Social Issues The clinic will also focus on reporting topics that shape communities and societies, including: Governance Human rights Social justice Civic participation Community development Participants will learn approaches for covering sensitive issues responsibly and effectively. Selection Criteria Applications will be evaluated using several key considerations. Demonstrated Interest in Journalism Applicants should show genuine enthusiasm for journalism, storytelling, media, and communication. Quality of Responses The application form includes written questions that allow applicants to demonstrate: Critical thinking Communication skills Understanding of journalism principles Strong, thoughtful responses will strengthen an application. Previous Media Experience Applicants with experience in: Campus media Student newspapers Blogging Storytelling Podcasting Content creation may have an advantage, although experience is not necessarily mandatory. Commitment to Ethical and Inclusive Journalism The programme prioritizes applicants who demonstrate a strong commitment to: Ethical reporting Diversity and inclusion Responsible journalism Community impact Potential for Community Impact Applicants should show how they intend to apply the knowledge gained to benefit: Their campuses Local communities Student audiences Broader society Gender Balance The programme also seeks balanced representation and encourages applications from diverse groups. Who Can Apply? This opportunity is specifically designed for students interested in journalism and media practice. Applicants must: Be students currently enrolled in a tertiary institution Have an interest in journalism, media, storytelling, or communication Be based in Ghana or The Gambia Be willing to fully participate in the programme Application Requirements Applicants will be required to provide information including: Personal Information Full name Email address Phone number Gender Nationality State or region Academic Information Name of institution Course of study Level or year of study Student Identification Applicants must upload: School ID card Or another valid form of student identification Journalism Experience Applicants will be asked about: Campus press membership Published content Journalism training attended Storytelling experience Written Responses Applicants must answer questions such as: Why they want to participate How ethical and inclusive journalism can improve society These responses play an important role in the selection process. Benefits of Participating Selected participants will gain: Practical Journalism Training Hands-on learning from experienced media professionals. Digital Journalism Skills Exposure to modern reporting tools and techniques. Networking Opportunities Connections with fellow student journalists and media practitioners. Enhanced Employability Valuable skills that strengthen future careers in: Journalism Communications Media Public relations Digital storytelling Development communication Mentorship Opportunities Access to guidance and support from journalism professionals. Application Deadline Monday, 8 June 2026 Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline. Apply Here Interested applicants can explore the Next Gen/Campus Reporter Project here Discover more training opportunities on OFY here The post Next Gen Campus Journalism Clinic 2026 for Student Journalists in Ghana and The Gambia: Fully-Focused Training Opportunity for Emerging Media Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Applications Open for the 2026 Global Survivor Council in Manila, Philippines: Join Global Advocacy Efforts to End Violence Against Children
summary: Brave Movement has officially opened applications for the 2026 Global Survivor Council (GSC) ahead of the highly anticipated 2nd Global Ministerial Conference on Ending Violence Against Children , which will take place in Manila . This important international opportunity seeks individuals from survivor-led networks and communities worldwide who are passionate about ending violence against children and ensuring survivor voices are represented in global policy discussions and advocacy initiatives. The Global Survivor Council will play a central role in shaping the design and delivery of the conference while strengthening survivor-led advocacy and international collaboration around child protection and violence prevention. Applications close on 10 June 2026 . About Brave Movement Brave Movement is a global survivor-led advocacy movement dedicated to ending childhood sexual violence and protecting children from abuse, exploitation, and violence. The movement works with survivors, advocates, policymakers, organizations, and communities around the world to: Raise awareness about violence against children Amplify survivor voices Advocate for prevention and protection policies Promote survivor-centered solutions Strengthen global accountability and action Brave Movement has become an important international platform for survivor leadership and advocacy in the movement to end childhood violence. About the Global Survivor Council (GSC) The Global Survivor Council (GSC) is a global body bringing together individuals from survivor-led networks and organizations worldwide who can contribute lived experience, advocacy expertise, and insights on different forms of violence against children. The Council is being coordinated by Brave Movement as part of preparations for the: 2nd Global Ministerial Conference on Ending Violence Against Children The Council will help ensure that survivor perspectives remain central to global conversations, policies, and conference activities. Purpose of the Global Survivor Council The Global Survivor Council aims to: Ensure survivor voices shape global advocacy discussions Contribute to conference planning and implementation Promote survivor-centered approaches Strengthen global collaboration among survivor-led movements Advance action to end violence against children worldwide The initiative recognizes that survivors play a critical role in shaping effective prevention, response, and protection strategies. Conference Location The 2nd Global Ministerial Conference on Ending Violence Against Children will take place in: Manila The conference will bring together: Governments International organizations Civil society groups Survivor advocates Researchers Youth leaders Child protection experts The goal is to strengthen international cooperation and action to prevent violence against children globally. Duration of the Global Survivor Council The Global Survivor Council will be active from: June 2026 – November 2026 Selected participants will engage in advocacy, consultations, planning discussions, and conference-related activities throughout this period. What the Global Survivor Council Will Do Council members will contribute to several important activities, including: 1. Survivor-Centered Advocacy Members will help ensure survivor experiences and perspectives are reflected in conference discussions and outcomes. 2. Global Consultation and Collaboration Participants will collaborate with survivor-led networks and international stakeholders from different regions of the world. 3. Conference Design and Delivery Council members may contribute ideas, feedback, and guidance related to: Conference themes Programming Sessions and discussions Advocacy priorities Survivor participation strategies 4. Strengthening Global Action The Council will help shape international conversations on: Child protection Violence prevention Survivor support Policy reform Advocacy initiatives Benefits of Participating Although membership on the Global Survivor Council is voluntary, selected members will receive important support and opportunities. Key Benefits Include: 1. Global Advocacy Experience Participants will contribute to a major international conference focused on ending violence against children. 2. International Networking Opportunities Members will connect with: Survivor advocates International organizations Policymakers Child protection experts Civil society leaders Youth advocates 3. Stipend Support Council members will receive: A stipend acknowledging their participation This support recognizes the time and contributions of selected members. 4. Travel Support For participants invited to attend the conference in Manila: Travel expenses will be covered Per diem support will be provided This helps ensure broader participation and accessibility for global members. Who Should Apply? The opportunity is open to individuals connected to survivor-led networks and advocacy communities worldwide. Applicants should ideally: Have lived experience relevant to violence against children advocacy Be engaged in survivor-led initiatives or networks Be passionate about child protection and violence prevention Be interested in global advocacy and policy discussions Be committed to survivor-centered approaches The Council seeks diverse global representation and perspectives. Why This Opportunity Matters Violence against children remains one of the world’s most urgent human rights and public health challenges. The Global Survivor Council represents an important step toward: Centering survivor leadership Strengthening accountability Promoting prevention Building inclusive policy solutions Supporting global advocacy movements By involving survivors directly in international dialogue and conference planning, the initiative aims to ensure more effective, informed, and compassionate responses to violence against children. Application Deadline Deadline: 10 June 2026 Interested applicants are encouraged to apply before the official closing date. How to Apply Interested individuals can learn more and apply through the official application link shared by Brave Movement: Apply Here Applicants can also learn more about the organization through here Discover more about this fellowship opportunity on here The post Applications Open for the 2026 Global Survivor Council in Manila, Philippines: Join Global Advocacy Efforts to End Violence Against Children appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: FCDO Senior Research Fellowships 2027: Fully Funded Two-Year Fellowship Opportunities Across 11 Global Research Areas
summary: The Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO), through the Global Research and Technology Development (GRTD) Research Commissioning Centre (RCC), has officially opened applications for eleven prestigious Senior Research Fellowships for 2027. These fellowships are designed to connect leading academic researchers with UK government policymaking and international development initiatives. The programme is co-led by the International Initiative for Impact Evaluation (3ie) and the University of Birmingham and offers experienced researchers the opportunity to contribute directly to high-impact global research and foreign policy priorities. Applications are now open, and interested candidates must submit their applications before 30 August 2026 at 5 PM GMT. About the FCDO Senior Research Fellowships The FCDO Senior Research Fellowships are aimed at senior academics who want to apply their expertise to real-world international challenges while collaborating closely with policymakers and government teams. The fellowships are part of the Global Research and Technology Development (GRTD) initiative, which supports evidence-based policymaking and strengthens the impact of research in development and foreign policy. Successful fellows will work with the FCDO on strategic research themes that influence global policy discussions, international development programmes, geopolitical strategies, technological advancement, and resilience planning. The placements will be hosted at the FCDO offices in London or East Kilbride. Fellowship Thematic Areas Applicants can apply under one of the following eleven thematic areas: Gender and Equity Conflict AI for Development Food and Agriculture Economic Development Systemic Resilience of Combined Human, Social, Economic, Political and Ecological Systems Hybrid and Transnational Threats Polar Regions Critical Minerals Technology, Geopolitics and Information Manipulation Global Health These themes reflect major global priorities shaping international development, diplomacy, technology, sustainability, and security. Fellowship Duration and Commitment The fellowship placement will run for: Two years 0.4 Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) This structure allows fellows to remain employed by their home academic institution while contributing substantially to FCDO-led research and policy initiatives. Fellowship Location Selected fellows will be hosted by the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) at: London, United Kingdom East Kilbride, United Kingdom Placement locations are subject to successful security clearance approval. Key Dates Important dates for applicants include: Call for Applications Opened: 20 May 2026 Application Deadline: 30 August 2026 at 5 PM GMT Interviews: Early October 2026 Fellowship Start Date: 1 April 2027 Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, meaning early application submission is strongly recommended. Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the FCDO Senior Research Fellowships, applicants must meet the following criteria: Academic Eligibility Applicants must: Be at least at Associate Professor level or equivalent seniority Be employed by a UK academic institution Remain employees of their sending institution throughout the fellowship period Security Clearance Requirements Applicants must be eligible to obtain UK security clearance. Depending on the fellowship role, applicants may require either: Developed Vetting (DV) Applicants must have physically lived in the UK for at least 5 of the last 10 years. Security Clearance (SC) Applicants must have physically lived in the UK for at least 2 of the last 5 years. The programme emphasizes that physical residency in the UK is mandatory. Maintaining a UK bank account or postal address alone does not satisfy residency requirements. Why This Fellowship Matters The FCDO Senior Research Fellowship programme offers a rare opportunity for experienced academics to bridge the gap between research and policymaking. Benefits of the fellowship include: Direct engagement with UK foreign policy and development initiatives Opportunity to shape international policy discussions Collaboration with government teams and policymakers Contribution to high-impact interdisciplinary research Access to strategic global development networks Enhanced professional visibility and influence Opportunity to work on globally significant challenges This fellowship is especially valuable for researchers interested in policy impact, international development, geopolitical strategy, technology governance, and global systems resilience. About the GRTD Research Commissioning Centre (RCC) The Research Commissioning Centre (RCC) was established to streamline and improve the commissioning and management of impactful research for the FCDO. The RCC helps reduce administrative barriers in research funding and delivery while ensuring high-quality, policy-relevant research outcomes. The centre is jointly led by: 3ie (International Initiative for Impact Evaluation) University of Birmingham Together, these institutions support evidence-informed policymaking and development impact worldwide. Required Policies and Compliance Shortlisted and selected fellows will be required to comply with several FCDO and 3ie policies, including: FCDO Senior Research Fellowship Agreement Template FCDO Programme Operating Framework (PrOF) Conflict of Interest and Publication Guidance 3ie Safeguarding Policy 3ie Fraud and Anti-Corruption Policy 3ie Transparent, Reproducible, and Ethical Evidence (TREE) Policy Applicants are strongly encouraged to review these policies carefully before submitting their applications. How to Apply Interested applicants should: Review the detailed job description for their selected thematic area Complete the online application form through the RCC Grant Management System Review the available user guide for the application system Prepare supporting academic and professional documentation Submit their application before the deadline Applications submitted early may receive faster consideration due to rolling reviews. Final Thoughts The FCDO Senior Research Fellowships 2027 represent one of the most significant opportunities for senior academics seeking to contribute directly to global policymaking, international development, technological governance, and geopolitical strategy. With placements across eleven high-priority global themes, the programme provides an exceptional platform for experienced researchers to influence policy while advancing impactful interdisciplinary research. Eligible academics interested in shaping global development and international affairs are encouraged to apply before the 30 August 2026 deadline. Official Application Link Applicants can access the official application portal and full fellowship details through the Global Research and Technology Development (GRTD) platform. Official Website: https://www.grtdprogramme.org/ Learn more and apply Click HERE to learn more and apply for the FCDO Senior Research Fellowships 2027. Explore more fully funded PhD opportunities on OFY HERE . The post FCDO Senior Research Fellowships 2027: Fully Funded Two-Year Fellowship Opportunities Across 11 Global Research Areas appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: AIGC for Future Global Challenge 2026: Win USD 10,000 and Showcase Your AI-Generated Creativity on a Global Stage in China and Switzerland
summary: The AIGC for Future Global Challenge 2026 is now officially open for applications, inviting young creators, innovators, researchers, interdisciplinary teams, startups, and organizations worldwide to explore how Artificial Intelligence Generated Content (AIGC) can help shape a more sustainable, inclusive, and innovative future. Co-hosted by the Global SDGs and Leadership Development Center (GSLDC) , the International Telecommunication Union , and the Global Alliance of Cities for the Digital Economy (DEC40) , this prestigious international challenge brings together global talent at the intersection of artificial intelligence, creativity, digital innovation, and sustainable development. The competition will take place in July 2026 across: Beijing Geneva Participants will have the opportunity to showcase innovative AI-generated works, compete for a total prize pool of USD 10,000 , gain international recognition, and connect with global leaders in technology, sustainability, and creative industries. The extended application deadline is 06 June 2025 at 11:59 PM Beijing Time . About the AIGC for Future Global Challenge The AIGC for Future Global Challenge is an international initiative developed under the broader AI for Future framework , designed to encourage responsible, impactful, and creative applications of artificial intelligence technologies. The challenge specifically focuses on the use of: AI-generated images AI-generated videos AI-generated audio Digital creative storytelling Innovative AI-driven communication Participants are encouraged to connect artistic creativity with real-world social impact aligned with the: United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) The challenge aims to empower creators to imagine and design solutions that contribute to a better future through digital innovation and emerging AI technologies. Organizers and Global Partners The challenge is co-hosted by several globally recognized organizations: Global SDGs and Leadership Development Center (GSLDC) International Telecommunication Union Global Alliance of Cities for the Digital Economy (DEC40) These organizations are working together to promote innovation, digital inclusion, youth leadership, and sustainable technological advancement worldwide. Competition Themes Participants are invited to submit AI-generated projects aligned with one or more important global themes. Main Focus Areas Include: 1. Digital Culture Projects exploring how AI can enhance creativity, storytelling, cultural preservation, digital expression, and artistic innovation. 2. Digital Consumption Ideas and creative works that rethink digital lifestyles, sustainable consumer behavior, digital commerce, and future economic interactions. 3. Women Empowerment Projects supporting gender equality, women’s participation in technology, leadership, education, entrepreneurship, and social inclusion. 4. Green Development AI-driven concepts focused on: Climate action Sustainability Renewable energy Environmental protection Circular economy Green innovation Accepted Submission Formats Participants may submit works in various AIGC formats, including: Images Videos Audio projects This flexibility allows creators to fully explore the possibilities of AI-generated creativity and interdisciplinary storytelling. Why You Should Apply The AIGC for Future Global Challenge offers much more than just prize money. It is an opportunity to gain international visibility and engage with global innovators. Key Benefits Include: 1. Global Recognition Participants can showcase their work before international institutions, global experts, technology leaders, and creative communities. Outstanding projects may receive significant international visibility. 2. International Networking Opportunities Applicants will have opportunities to connect with: AI innovators Researchers Creators Policymakers Technology experts Sustainability leaders The challenge promotes interdisciplinary collaboration and long-term partnerships. 3. Real-World Impact and Transformation Selected projects may move beyond the competition into: Public exhibitions Communication campaigns Commercial pathways Cross-border collaborations Broader implementation opportunities This creates possibilities for long-term visibility and real-world application. 4. Awards and Financial Support Participants will compete for: USD 10,000 Prize Pool Winning participants may also receive: Promotion opportunities Collaboration opportunities Development support International exposure 5. International Exchange Opportunities Selected participants may participate in international exchange visits linked to major global platforms such as: Global Digital Economy Conference AI for Good Global Summit These exchanges may include activities in: Beijing, China Geneva, Switzerland 6. Creative Freedom and Innovation The challenge encourages participants to explore AI creativity with flexibility in: Format Style Storytelling Technical experimentation Interdisciplinary collaboration Creators are encouraged to think boldly while addressing meaningful global challenges. Eligibility Requirements The competition is open to a wide range of participants worldwide. Applicants Must: Be between 18–40 years old Apply individually or in teams Teams may include up to seven members Be creators, innovators, companies, research institutions, or interdisciplinary groups Demonstrate creativity and alignment with the competition themes The programme strongly encourages: Cross-disciplinary collaboration International cooperation Creative experimentation Important Submission Rules Participants should carefully review the official submission requirements before applying. Key Rules Include: 1. Team Representative Requirement Only the designated Team Representative can submit the application on behalf of the team. The representative is responsible for ensuring: Accurate participant information Correct proposal details Proper consent from all members 2. Submission Limits Each participant, organization, or team may: Submit up to three entries total Submit only one entry at a time Applicants may participate across multiple competition tracks provided all submissions meet the technical and thematic guidelines. 3. Final Submission Rule Once an application is submitted: It cannot be edited Applicants should therefore carefully review all information before submission. Programme Timeline Activity Date Application Deadline 06 June 2025 Competition Activities July 2026 Host Locations Beijing Geneva How to Apply All applications must be submitted through the official submission form provided by the organizers. Participants are encouraged to review the official programme documents and proposal criteria carefully before applying. Applicants can also join the official WhatsApp group for updates and discussions: Official WhatsApp Group Contact Email For inquiries: aigc4future@ungyldc.org Apply Here Why This Opportunity Matters Artificial Intelligence Generated Content (AIGC) is rapidly transforming industries worldwide, including: Education Entertainment Media Healthcare Climate innovation Communication Public awareness Creative arts This challenge provides a platform for young innovators to demonstrate how AI can be used responsibly and creatively to address global issues and advance sustainable development goals. It also creates opportunities for emerging creators from around the world to contribute meaningfully to the future of technology and society. Application Deadline Extended Deadline: 06 June 2025 (11:59 PM Beijing Time) Interested participants are strongly encouraged to apply before the deadline. For more information on this competition visit here Discover more opportunities on OFY, click here The post AIGC for Future Global Challenge 2026: Win USD 10,000 and Showcase Your AI-Generated Creativity on a Global Stage in China and Switzerland appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: MEST AI Startup Program 2027: Fully Immersive Opportunity for Aspiring African AI Entrepreneurs to Build and Scale High-Impact Startups
summary: The MEST AI Startup Program 2027 is now accepting applications from ambitious and innovative African entrepreneurs who are passionate about artificial intelligence, technology, and startup creation. Organized by MEST Africa , this highly competitive program offers a unique opportunity for talented individuals to transform innovative AI-driven ideas into scalable businesses capable of solving real-world challenges across Africa and beyond. Designed as an intensive entrepreneurial training and incubation experience, the MEST AI Startup Program aims to identify and equip Africa’s most promising AI innovators with the technical, business, and leadership skills required to launch globally competitive startups. Applications are open until 20 July 2026 . About the MEST AI Startup Program The MEST AI Startup Program is an immersive entrepreneurial journey that combines hands-on training, mentorship, startup development, and venture incubation. The program is specifically designed for aspiring AI entrepreneurs who want to: Build innovative AI solutions Launch scalable technology startups Develop market-ready products Gain entrepreneurial and leadership skills Access mentorship from global experts Join a growing network of African innovators Participants work closely with experienced entrepreneurs, software developers, AI specialists, investors, and industry leaders throughout the program. Why the MEST AI Startup Program Matters Artificial Intelligence is rapidly transforming industries worldwide, creating opportunities for entrepreneurs to develop solutions that address challenges in sectors such as: Agriculture Healthcare Education Financial Services Climate Innovation Logistics E-commerce Governance The MEST AI Startup Program empowers African innovators to take advantage of these opportunities while creating solutions tailored to local and global markets. By focusing on practical startup development and commercialization, the program helps bridge the gap between technological innovation and sustainable business growth. Program Structure The MEST AI Startup Program runs for up to 12 months and consists of two major phases. Phase 1: Intensive Training Program Duration January 2027 – July 2027 (7 Months) During the training phase, participants will engage in rigorous, hands-on learning experiences designed to help them build strong startup foundations. Participants will: Explore and Refine AI Business Ideas Identify market opportunities Validate customer needs Refine startup concepts Develop innovative AI solutions Build an AI Minimum Viable Product (MVP) Participants will learn how to: Design products Develop software solutions Test ideas with users Deploy AI-powered applications Learn AI Development Skills The training provides exposure to: Artificial Intelligence technologies Machine Learning concepts AI product development Software engineering practices Gain Business and Startup Knowledge Participants will also receive instruction in: Business modeling Lean startup methodologies Customer discovery Product-market fit Go-to-market strategies Venture growth Work with Global Experts The program connects participants with: Experienced entrepreneurs AI practitioners Startup founders Industry mentors Technology leaders At the end of the training phase, participants will pitch their ventures for consideration into the incubation stage. Phase 2: Startup Incubation Duration September 2027 – December 2027 (4 Months) Only the most promising startups emerging from the training phase will be selected for incubation. During incubation, startups will focus on: Product Scaling Participants will: Improve product functionality Enhance user experience Expand customer acquisition efforts Business Model Validation Startups will: Test revenue models Validate market demand Improve operational systems Investor Readiness Participants will receive support in: Fundraising preparation Investment strategy development Pitch refinement Investor engagement Market Expansion Teams will work toward: Growing their user base Building commercial traction Establishing scalable operations The ultimate goal is to prepare startups for investment opportunities and long-term growth. What Participants Will Gain The MEST AI Startup Program provides significant value for entrepreneurs seeking to build successful ventures. Technical Skills Participants gain experience in: AI software development Product design MVP creation Technology deployment Entrepreneurial Skills Training includes: Startup strategy Business planning Market validation Customer acquisition Revenue generation Mentorship and Networking Participants receive guidance from: Industry experts Successful founders Investors Technology leaders Startup Incubation Opportunities High-performing teams may receive continued support to scale their businesses through the incubation phase. Access to a Pan-African Entrepreneurial Ecosystem Participants become part of the broader MEST community, which includes: Founders Investors Mentors Corporate partners Startup ecosystems across Africa Application Process The application process involves several stages. Step 1: Eligibility Screening Applicants first complete an eligibility assessment to confirm they meet the program requirements. Step 2: Full Application Form Eligible candidates will submit: Personal information Professional background Skills and experiences A short personal statement video Step 3: Skills Assessment Applicants must complete an assessment designed to evaluate: Technical competencies Business acumen Problem-solving abilities Interpersonal skills Step 4: In-Person Interviews and Group Challenge Shortlisted candidates will participate in: Interviews Team exercises Group challenges These assessments will take place in: Ghana Nigeria Senegal Kenya Step 5: Contract Offer Successful applicants will receive admission offers and contracts for participation. Eligibility Requirements To apply for the MEST AI Startup Program, applicants must meet the following requirements. Educational Qualification Applicants should possess: A Bachelor’s degree; OR A Diploma; OR Equivalent practical experience Age Requirement Applicants must be: 21–35 years old Technical Experience Candidates should demonstrate: Software development experience Basic understanding of Artificial Intelligence Strong technical aptitude Entrepreneurial Interest Applicants should possess: Interest in startup creation Innovative thinking Problem-solving abilities Desire to build scalable businesses Citizenship Requirement Applicants must be citizens of: West Africa or East Africa Applicants from countries outside the primary recruitment countries may still apply. However, shortlisted candidates must be able to travel at their own expense to one of the interview locations if selected. Commitment Requirement Participants must be willing to: Fully commit to the training period Participate in incubation activities if selected Pursue entrepreneurship as a full-time career path Preferred Qualifications The following are advantageous but not mandatory: Experience working in cross-functional teams AI innovation experience Entrepreneurial background Interest in global markets Cross-cultural collaboration skills Important Dates Date Activity 19 May 2026 Applications Open 20 July 2026 Application Deadline September 2026 Shortlist Notifications October 2026 Interviews and Group Challenges Mid-October 2026 Contract Offers November – December 2026 Virtual Pre-Learning January 2027 Program Begins in Ghana August 2027 Training Graduation September – December 2027 Startup Incubation January 2028 Portfolio Onboarding for Selected Ventures How to Apply Interested applicants should complete their application through the official application portal. Application Deadline 20 July 2026 (11:59 PM GMT) Apply Here Questions? Applicants may contact: recruitment@meltwater.org For additional support, applicants may also access MEST’s FAQ resources and WhatsApp assistance channels. Why You Should Apply The MEST AI Startup Program is one of Africa’s most exciting opportunities for aspiring entrepreneurs interested in Artificial Intelligence and startup creation. By combining world-class training, venture development, mentorship, and startup incubation, the program provides a strong foundation for innovators seeking to build impactful and globally competitive AI businesses. For young Africans passionate about technology, entrepreneurship, and solving real-world challenges through AI, this program offers a powerful pathway to turn ideas into successful ventures. For more information on this opportunity visit here Discover more opportunities on OFY, click here The post MEST AI Startup Program 2027: Fully Immersive Opportunity for Aspiring African AI Entrepreneurs to Build and Scale High-Impact Startups appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: LEGO Foundation Fellowship 2027: $300,000 Global Research Fellowship for Early- and Mid-Career Scholars Working on Children’s Thriving
summary: Applications are now open for the LEGO Foundation Fellowship 2027, a prestigious global research fellowship supporting early- and mid-career researchers whose work advances understanding of how children thrive across diverse contexts worldwide. Administered by the Social Science Research Council (SSRC) in partnership with the LEGO Foundation, this highly competitive fellowship provides flexible funding of USD 300,000 over three years to scholars conducting ambitious, rigorous, and practically relevant research related to children’s wellbeing, development, learning, inclusion, resilience, and thriving. The fellowship brings together researchers from multiple disciplines including education, psychology, child development, sociology, neuroscience, public health, data science, humanitarian studies, disability studies, implementation science, economics, and human-computer interaction. Researchers selected for the fellowship will not only receive substantial financial support but will also become part of a global interdisciplinary cohort dedicated to generating evidence that informs real-world action for children. Applications must be submitted by July 31, 2026 . About the LEGO Foundation Fellowship The LEGO Foundation Fellowship is a global initiative created to support researchers whose work can strengthen understanding of how children learn, grow, develop, and thrive in complex and changing environments. The fellowship reflects the LEGO Foundation’s long-standing commitment to improving children’s lives worldwide through education, creativity, learning through play, wellbeing, and inclusive development. The programme coincides with the LEGO Foundation’s 40th anniversary and responds to urgent global challenges affecting children today, including: Humanitarian crises and conflict Displacement and instability Mental health challenges Educational inequalities Neurodivergence and inclusion The rapid expansion of artificial intelligence Social disconnection and reduced learning engagement The fellowship is designed to empower researchers who are producing evidence capable of shaping policy, practice, and systems that better support children globally. Fellowship Funding and Benefits Selected fellows will receive extensive support over a three-year period. Fellowship Benefits Include: 1. USD 300,000 Flexible Research Funding Each fellow receives: USD 300,000 over three years Funding inclusive of 15% indirect costs Institution-administered funding support The fellowship funding can be used for: Research personnel Professional travel Equipment Data collection Dissemination activities Trainee support Research-related operational costs IRB-related costs Fellowship meeting travel expenses 2. Global Fellowship Community Fellows join an international interdisciplinary network of researchers focused on children’s thriving. Participants gain: Global collaboration opportunities Research exchange platforms Peer learning experiences Interdisciplinary dialogue Networking with policy and practice audiences 3. Annual Convenings and Cohort Activities The fellowship includes: Annual meetings Virtual cohort sessions Research-sharing opportunities Engagement with practitioners and policymakers 4. International Visibility Fellows receive: Recognition within the LEGO Foundation network Media visibility opportunities Dissemination support for research outputs 5. Long-Term Career Advancement The programme supports researchers in: Strengthening leadership capacity Advancing impactful research agendas Translating evidence into practice Building interdisciplinary partnerships Research Themes for the Fellowship Applicants must align their proposed research with one or more of the fellowship’s three core themes. Theme 1: The Youngest Children in Crisis and Conflict Settings This theme focuses on children from birth to age eight living in crisis, displacement, or humanitarian settings. Research areas include: Mechanisms of Resilience and Pathways to Thriving Studies exploring how children continue to develop and learn despite adversity. Strengthening Caregiving and Family Environments Research on parenting, caregiving, and family systems supporting child development. Scaling and Sustaining Effective Interventions Evidence on how child development and mental health interventions can be expanded and sustained. Translating Evidence into Practice Research examining how evidence-based interventions are adopted and implemented in humanitarian settings. The programme welcomes studies involving: Refugee children Internally displaced children Children in humanitarian emergencies Children in recovery contexts Vulnerable host community children Theme 2: Inclusion and Wellbeing of Neurodivergent Children This theme focuses especially on autism and ADHD. Research may explore: Support Before Diagnosis How neurodivergent children can receive support before formal assessment or diagnosis. Strengthening Family Environments The role of home routines, caregiving, and family wellbeing. Inclusive Learning Environments How education systems and classroom practices can better support neurodivergent learners. Transitions and Continuity of Support Research on maintaining support during educational and developmental transitions. Enabling Tools Evidence on how digital technologies reduce barriers to communication, participation, and learning. Theme 3: Children’s Learning and Development in an AI-Enabled World This theme explores how artificial intelligence is reshaping children’s experiences and development. Research topics include: AI and Adult-Child Connection Understanding how AI influences relationships between children and caregivers. AI and Social-Emotional Development Research on how AI affects emotional wellbeing, social interaction, and help-seeking behaviors. AI and Productive Struggle in Learning Studies examining AI’s effect on persistence, engagement, and motivation. Differential Effects of AI Evidence on whether AI reduces or deepens educational inequalities. Is Play Required in the Research Proposal? No. Applicants may include play where relevant, but it is not mandatory and is not a condition for eligibility. Who Can Apply? The fellowship is open to early- and mid-career researchers worldwide. Applicants Must: Hold a PhD or equivalent research doctorate by July 31, 2026 Have received their PhD no earlier than January 1, 2016 Be employed by a university or research institute Submit an individual application Propose research aligned with at least one fellowship theme Have institutional capacity to administer the grant The programme also recognizes documented career breaks such as: Parental leave Caregiving responsibilities Illness Displacement Other research interruptions Eligible Disciplines Researchers from diverse academic backgrounds are encouraged to apply, including: Education Psychology Public health Sociology Neuroscience Economics Data science Disability studies Humanitarian studies Child development Human-computer interaction Implementation science Countries Eligible to Apply Applicants from all countries are welcome except those subject to EU or US sanctions.
